axis: cache hit rate (hardware/model-independent) — the metric SGLang's paper headlines; fak runs the same radix-tree + longest-prefix + LRU-leaf algorithm, so on the same workload it reaches the same reuse
engine: fak radixbench — RadixAttention-style prefix cache (internal/radixkv) over the kernel-owned KV cache
notes: baseline=full prefill per request; declare-one-prefix=fak's pre-radix single-declared-prefix reuse; radix=internal/radixkv automatic longest-prefix discovery (incl. mid-run split). Live arm is a real kernel prefill per request, bit-identical to recompute (proven in internal/radixkv). Cache-aware order = lexicographic (== DFS == longest-shared-prefix-first), reproducing the paper's optimal-hit-rate-at-budget>=maxlen result.

policy_eviction.note: verdict-driven span eviction (radixkv.EvictNode); shared system prefix + benign sibling preserved. LRU radix caches evict only under memory pressure.

workloads.name: agents
workloads.desc: C concurrent agents share a system prefix; each has a private growing context, arrivals interleaved (ReAct shape)
workloads.sglang_published: two-level reuse (shared system + per-agent chain); cache-aware scheduling recovers hit rate under interleaving
